reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Gate 3 (Code Verification): Phase 03's code reading is factually correct. The handler blobSidecarByRootRPCHandler (beacon-chain/sync/rpc_blob_sidecars_by_root.go:26-109) is registered in the Fulu fork map (rpc.go:52) but lacks the required epoch-based deprecation guard. The handler only checks retention window via BlobRPCMinValidSlot (which uses DenebForkEpoch as lower bound), and contains no logic to reject requests when current_epoch >= FULU_FORK_EPOCH + MIN_EPOCHS_FOR_BLOB_SIDECARS_REQUESTS (4096 epochs). Gate 4 (Exploitability): Attacker control is direct—any P2P peer can send blob_sidecars_by_root requests after the deprecation deadline. An attacker can trigger this via P2P by sending blob_sidecars_by_root RPC requests for blobs within the retention window but after FULU_FORK_EPOCH + 4096, causing the node to serve legacy blob data instead of rejecting per spec, delaying PeerDAS migration and enabling resource exhaustion via deprecated RPC paths. Gate 5 (Spec Cross-Reference): 01e property PROP-6a4369e9-inv-035 from outputs/01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json line 896-911 requires: 'blob_sidecars_by_root must be fully deprecated after FULU_FORK_EPOCH + MIN_EPOCHS' with assertion 'current_epoch >= FULU_FORK_EPOCH + MIN_EPOCHS_FOR_BLOB_SIDECARS_REQUESTS => respond_empty_or_reject'. Severity: Original MEDIUM severity is justified—this is a Fusaka-specific spec compliance issue affecting Prysm (31% CL market share). The impact (delayed PeerDAS migration + resource exhaustion) affects >5% of network if Prysm nodes fail to deprecate the endpoint, meeting the MEDIUM threshold per BUG_BOUNTY_SCOPE.json severity_classification. Practical exploitability depends on timing (must occur after FULU_FORK_EPOCH + 4096) and sustained peer requests during the deprecation window, warranting CONFIRMED_POTENTIAL rather than CONFIRMED_VULNERABILITY.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: blob_sidecars_by_root must be fully deprecated after FULU_FORK_EPOCH + MIN_EPOCHS.
